title: Cyclophorus stigmosus
uses: Cyclophorus stigmosus, a species of air-breathing land snail, has been found to have various medicinal properties in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia. The snail's mucus is believed to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin infections. In some cultures, the snail's mucus is also used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Cyclophorus stigmosus for medicinal purposes. More studies are required to fully understand its potential health benefits and applications.
taxonomy: Cyclophorus stigmosus belongs to the family Cyclophoridae. This species of snail is a type of small land snail. Cyclophorus stigmosus is further classified under the subfamily Cyclophorinae. It is a part of the order Stylommatophora. The classification is based on its shell structure and other morphological features.

intro: Cyclophorus stigmosus, commonly known as the spotted Cyclophorus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyclophoridae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclophorus stigmosus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cyclophorus stigmosus is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture to treat respiratory infections and other conditions, with the latter being more commonly used in traditional folk medicine.
